title: Transcribe Audio (Candle)
cap_description: Transcribe audio files to text using Whisper models with Candle ML framework
documentation:
## Purpose

Converts spoken audio into text using OpenAI Whisper models running locally via the Candle ML framework. Runs on CPU or GPU without a network dependency after the model is downloaded.

## Inputs

- A WAV audio file containing speech (mono or stereo, any sample rate Whisper supports).
- An optional language code (e.g. `en`, `fr`, `zh`). Omit to let Whisper auto-detect the language.
- An optional timestamps flag. When enabled, each segment in the output record includes start and end times.
- An optional model spec pointing to a HuggingFace Whisper checkpoint. Defaults to `openai/whisper-base`.

## Outputs

A JSON record containing the full transcript text, per-segment breakdown, detected language, and model metadata.

## Notes

- Larger Whisper models (`whisper-small`, `whisper-medium`, `whisper-large-v3`) produce more accurate transcripts at the cost of memory and latency.
- Input must be WAV. Convert other audio formats upstream before passing to this cap.
- See also: [Generate Vector Embeddings (Candle)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:candle;in="media:textable";ml-model;op=generate_embeddings;out="media:embedding-vector;textable;record") to embed the resulting transcript.

args.arg_description: Path to the audio file (WAV format)
Language code for transcription
Include timestamps in output
Whisper model name from HuggingFace

output.output_description: Transcribed text with optional timestamps and metadata

title: Coerce Boolean Array to String
cap_description: Convert an array of booleans to its string representation
documentation:
## Purpose

Converts a list of boolean values to a single string representation (e.g. `[true, false, true]`). Use this when a downstream cap expects a text value but the upstream produces a boolean array.

## Inputs

A list of boolean values (media type `media:bool;list;textable`).

## Outputs

A string representation of the boolean array.

## Notes

- Type adapter for the capfab planner.
- For converting individual booleans, use [Coerce Boolean to String](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:bool;textable";op=coerce;out=media:textable).

args.arg_description: Input value to coerce

title: Coerce Boolean to Integer
cap_description: Convert a boolean to an integer (true=1, false=0)
documentation:
## Purpose

Converts a boolean value to an integer: `true` becomes `1`, `false` becomes `0`. Use this when a downstream cap expects an integer flag or count but the upstream produces a boolean.

## Inputs

A boolean value (media type `media:bool;textable`).

## Outputs

The integer `1` (true) or `0` (false).

## Notes

- Standard boolean-to-int mapping used across most programming contexts.
- To widen further to a float, chain with [Coerce Integer to Number](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:integer;numeric;textable";op=coerce;out="media:numeric;textable").

args.arg_description: Input value to coerce

title: Coerce Boolean to Number
cap_description: Convert a boolean to a number (true=1.0, false=0.0)
documentation:
## Purpose

Converts a boolean value to a floating-point number: `true` becomes `1.0`, `false` becomes `0.0`. Use this when a downstream cap expects a numeric type but the upstream produces a boolean.

## Inputs

A boolean value (media type `media:bool;textable`).

## Outputs

The float `1.0` (true) or `0.0` (false).

## Notes

- Use when connecting boolean outputs to numeric inputs (e.g. weight or scaling factors).
- For integer output instead, use [Coerce Boolean to Integer](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:bool;textable";op=coerce;out="media:integer;numeric;textable").

args.arg_description: Input value to coerce

title: Coerce Boolean to Object
cap_description: Wrap a boolean value in a record
documentation:
## Purpose

Wraps a boolean value in a record (object) so it can be passed to caps that expect a structured record input. The boolean is stored under a standard key in the output record.

## Inputs

A boolean value (media type `media:bool;textable`).

## Outputs

A record containing the boolean value.

## Notes

- Use when a downstream cap requires a record type but the upstream produces a scalar boolean.
- This is primarily a type adapter for the capfab planner.

args.arg_description: Input value to coerce

title: Coerce Boolean to String
cap_description: Convert a boolean value to its string representation
documentation:
## Purpose

Converts a boolean media value to its textable string form (`"true"` or `"false"`). Use this when a downstream cap requires a string type but the upstream cap produces a boolean.

## Inputs

A boolean value (media type `media:bool;textable`).

## Outputs

The string `"true"` or `"false"`.

## Notes

- This is a type-level adapter for the capfab planner. The planner inserts coerce caps automatically when a boolean output needs to connect to a string input.
- For the reverse, see [Coerce String to Integer](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in=media:textable;op=coerce;out="media:integer;numeric;textable") or parse the string downstream.

args.arg_description: Input value to coerce

title: Coerce Integer Array to String
cap_description: Convert an array of integers to its string representation
documentation:
## Purpose

Converts a list of integers to a single string representation. Use this when a downstream cap expects a text value but the upstream produces an integer array.

## Inputs

A list of integer values (media type `media:integer;list;numeric;textable`).

## Outputs

A string representation of the integer array.

## Notes

- Type adapter for the capfab planner.
- For converting individual integers, use [Coerce Integer to String](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:integer;numeric;textable";op=coerce;out=media:textable).

args.arg_description: Input value to coerce

title: Coerce Integer to Number
cap_description: Widen an integer to a floating-point number
documentation:
## Purpose

Widens an integer to a floating-point number. Use this when a downstream cap requires a numeric (float) type but the upstream produces an integer.

## Inputs

An integer value (media type `media:integer;numeric;textable`).

## Outputs

The same value as a floating-point number.

## Notes

- Widening conversion — no precision is lost for integers that fit in a 64-bit float (which is all practical integer values).
- For the narrowing direction (float to int), use [Coerce Number to Integer](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:numeric;textable";op=coerce;out="media:integer;numeric;textable") which truncates.

title: Coerce Integer to String
cap_description: Convert an integer value to its string representation
documentation:
## Purpose

Converts an integer to its decimal string representation. Use this when a downstream cap requires a text value but the upstream produces an integer.

## Inputs

An integer value (media type `media:integer;numeric;textable`).

## Outputs

The decimal string representation of the integer (e.g. `42` becomes `"42"`).

## Notes

- No locale-specific formatting — output is always plain decimal digits.
- For the reverse conversion, use [Coerce String to Integer](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in=media:textable;op=coerce;out="media:integer;numeric;textable").

args.arg_description: Input value to coerce

title: Coerce Number Array to String
cap_description: Convert an array of numbers to its string representation
documentation:
## Purpose

Converts a list of floating-point numbers to a single string representation. Use this when a downstream cap expects text but the upstream produces a numeric array.

## Inputs

A list of numeric values (media type `media:list;numeric;textable`).

## Outputs

A string representation of the number array.

## Notes

- Type adapter for the capfab planner.
- For converting individual numbers, use [Coerce Number to String](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:numeric;textable";op=coerce;out=media:textable).

args.arg_description: Input value to coerce

title: Coerce Number to Integer
cap_description: Truncate a numeric value to an integer
documentation:
## Purpose

Truncates a floating-point number to an integer by discarding the fractional part (toward zero). Use this when a downstream cap requires an integer but the upstream produces a float.

## Inputs

A numeric (float) value (media type `media:numeric;textable`).

## Outputs

The integer part of the number (fractional part discarded, not rounded).

## Notes

- Truncation, not rounding: `3.9` becomes `3`, `-3.9` becomes `-3`.
- For the widening direction (no precision loss), use [Coerce Integer to Number](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:integer;numeric;textable";op=coerce;out="media:numeric;textable").

title: Coerce Number to String
cap_description: Convert a numeric value to its string representation
documentation:
## Purpose

Converts a floating-point number to its string representation. Use this when a downstream cap requires a text value but the upstream produces a numeric value.

## Inputs

A numeric (float) value (media type `media:numeric;textable`).

## Outputs

The string representation of the number.

## Notes

- Output format follows standard floating-point decimal notation.
- For the reverse conversion, use [Coerce String to Number](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in=media:textable;op=coerce;out="media:numeric;textable").

title: Coerce Object Array to String
cap_description: Convert an array of records to its string representation
documentation:
## Purpose

Converts a list of records to a single string representation. Use this when a downstream cap expects text but the upstream produces a list of objects.

## Inputs

A list of records (media type `media:list;record`).

## Outputs

A string representation of the record array.

## Notes

- Type adapter for the capfab planner.
- Output is typically serialized as a JSON array.
- For converting individual records, use [Coerce Object to String](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in=media:record;op=coerce;out=media:textable).

args.arg_description: Input value to coerce

title: Coerce Object to String
cap_description: Convert a record/object to its string representation
documentation:
## Purpose

Converts a record (object) to its string representation. Use this when a downstream cap requires text but the upstream produces a structured record.

## Inputs

A record value (media type `media:record`).

## Outputs

A string representation of the record.

## Notes

- The output format is typically JSON or a similar serialized form.
- For wrapping a string in a record (the reverse), use [Coerce String to Object](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in=media:textable;op=coerce;out=media:record).
- For human-readable text output from a record, a format conversion cap (JSON-to-YAML, etc.) may produce better output than this coerce.

args.arg_description: Input value to coerce

title: Coerce String Array to String
cap_description: Convert a list of strings to a single string representation
documentation:
## Purpose

Converts a list of strings to a single string representation. Use this when a downstream cap expects a single text value but the upstream produces multiple strings.

## Inputs

A list of textable strings (media type `media:list;textable`).

## Outputs

A single string representing all items in the list (typically newline-separated or JSON array format).

## Notes

- Type adapter for the capfab planner.
- If you need the items joined in a specific format (comma-separated, newline-separated, etc.), consider using a more explicit join operation upstream.

title: Coerce String to Integer
cap_description: Parse a string value as an integer
documentation:
## Purpose

Parses a text string as an integer. Use this when a string-producing cap outputs a number as text (e.g. from a file or LLM output) and a downstream cap requires an integer type.

## Inputs

A textable string value representing an integer (media type `media:textable`).

## Outputs

The parsed integer.

## Notes

- Fails hard if the input is not a valid integer string. Ensure upstream output is numeric text before using this coerce.
- For floating-point strings, use [Coerce String to Number](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in=media:textable;op=coerce;out="media:numeric;textable") instead.

args.arg_description: Input value to coerce

title: Coerce String to Number
cap_description: Parse a string value as a number
documentation:
## Purpose

Parses a text string as a floating-point number. Use this when a string-producing cap outputs a numeric value as text and a downstream cap requires a numeric type.

## Inputs

A textable string representing a number (media type `media:textable`).

## Outputs

The parsed floating-point number.

## Notes

- Fails hard if the string is not a valid numeric value.
- For integers specifically, use [Coerce String to Integer](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in=media:textable;op=coerce;out="media:integer;numeric;textable").
- For the reverse, use [Coerce Number to String](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:numeric;textable";op=coerce;out=media:textable).

title: Merge CSV Files
cap_description: Merge multiple CSV files into a single CSV file
documentation:
## Purpose

Merges a sequence of CSV files into a single CSV file. Headers from the first file are used; subsequent files contribute their data rows. Use this to fan-in results from parallel processing stages that each produce a CSV.

## Inputs

A sequence of CSV files (each with a header row and one or more data rows).

## Outputs

A single merged CSV file containing all rows from all inputs, with a single header.

## Notes

- All input CSVs must have compatible headers (same columns in the same order).
- Output preserves the row order of the inputs (first input rows appear first).
- For cross-format merging (CSV inputs to JSON or YAML output), use [Merge CSV Files into JSON Array](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:csv;list;record;textable";op=collect_records;out="media:json;list;record;textable") or [Merge CSV Files into YAML List](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:csv;list;record;textable";op=collect_records;out="media:list;record;textable;yaml").

args.arg_description: CSV files to merge

output.output_description: Single CSV file with all rows merged

title: Merge CSV Files into JSON Array
cap_description: Merge multiple CSV files into a single JSON array of objects
documentation:
## Purpose

Merges a sequence of CSV files into a single JSON array of objects, with each row becoming a JSON object and column headers becoming keys. Use this when downstream processing prefers JSON over CSV.

## Inputs

A sequence of CSV files (each with a header row and data rows).

## Outputs

A JSON array where each element is an object with keys from the CSV header and values from the row.

## Notes

- All input CSVs should have compatible headers.
- Useful for feeding CSV data into JSON-consuming caps or APIs.
- For keeping the output as CSV, use [Merge CSV Files](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:csv;list;record;textable";op=collect_records;out="media:csv;list;record;textable").

args.arg_description: CSV files to merge

output.output_description: JSON array with all CSV rows as objects

title: Merge CSV Files into YAML List
cap_description: Merge multiple CSV files into a single YAML sequence of mappings
documentation:
## Purpose

Merges a sequence of CSV files into a single YAML sequence of mappings, with each row becoming a YAML mapping and column headers becoming keys. Use this when downstream processing prefers YAML.

## Inputs

A sequence of CSV files (each with a header row and data rows).

## Outputs

A YAML sequence where each element is a mapping with keys from the CSV header.

## Notes

- All input CSVs should have compatible headers.
- For JSON output, use [Merge CSV Files into JSON Array](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:csv;list;record;textable";op=collect_records;out="media:json;list;record;textable").

args.arg_description: CSV files to merge

output.output_description: YAML sequence with all CSV rows as mappings

title: Collect JSON Objects into Array
cap_description: Collect individual JSON objects into a single JSON array
documentation:
## Purpose

Gathers a sequence of individual JSON objects (one per pipeline item) into a single JSON array. Use this to fan-in results from parallel processing where each parallel branch produces one JSON object.

## Inputs

A sequence of JSON record objects.

## Outputs

A JSON array containing all input objects in order.

## Notes

- The objects do not need to have the same keys; heterogeneous objects are permitted.
- For CSV output, use [Collect JSON Objects into CSV](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:json;record;textable";op=collect_json_objects;out="media:csv;list;record;textable").
- For YAML output, use [Collect JSON Objects into YAML List](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:json;record;textable";op=collect_json_objects;out="media:list;record;textable;yaml").

args.arg_description: JSON objects to collect into an array

output.output_description: JSON array containing all input objects

title: Collect JSON Objects into CSV
cap_description: Collect individual JSON objects into a single CSV file
documentation:
## Purpose

Gathers a sequence of individual JSON objects into a single CSV file. Object keys become column headers; one row is written per object. Use this to produce tabular output from a fan-in of JSON-producing parallel stages.

## Inputs

A sequence of JSON record objects.

## Outputs

A CSV file with headers derived from the union of all object keys, and one row per object.

## Notes

- Objects with missing keys produce empty cells in those columns.
- Objects with different key sets are merged into a unified column set.
- For a JSON array output instead, use [Collect JSON Objects into Array](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:json;record;textable";op=collect_json_objects;out="media:json;list;record;textable").

args.arg_description: JSON objects to collect into a CSV

output.output_description: CSV file with one row per input object

title: Collect JSON Objects into YAML List
cap_description: Collect individual JSON objects into a single YAML sequence
documentation:
## Purpose

Gathers a sequence of individual JSON objects into a single YAML sequence of mappings. Each JSON object becomes one YAML mapping entry. Use this when downstream processing or configuration formats prefer YAML.

## Inputs

A sequence of JSON record objects.

## Outputs

A YAML sequence where each element is a mapping corresponding to one input JSON object.

## Notes

- Key order in the YAML output may differ from the JSON input; YAML mappings are unordered by spec.
- For a JSON array output, use [Collect JSON Objects into Array](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:json;record;textable";op=collect_json_objects;out="media:json;list;record;textable").

args.arg_description: JSON objects to collect into a YAML sequence

output.output_description: YAML sequence containing all input objects as mappings

title: Merge YAML Mappings into CSV
cap_description: Merge multiple YAML mapping files into a single CSV file
documentation:
## Purpose

Merges a sequence of YAML mapping files into a single CSV file. Each YAML mapping becomes one row; mapping keys become column headers. Use this to convert YAML-structured data from parallel processing stages into a tabular CSV for analysis or export.

## Inputs

A sequence of YAML mapping files (each file is a single YAML mapping, not a list).

## Outputs

A CSV file with headers from the union of all mapping keys and one row per input file.

## Notes

- Mappings with missing keys produce empty cells.
- For JSON array output, use [Merge YAML Mappings into JSON Array](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:record;textable;yaml";op=collect_records;out="media:json;list;record;textable").

args.arg_description: YAML mapping files to merge

output.output_description: CSV file with one row per YAML mapping

title: Merge YAML Mappings into JSON Array
cap_description: Merge multiple YAML mapping files into a single JSON array of objects
documentation:
## Purpose

Merges a sequence of YAML mapping files into a single JSON array of objects. Each YAML mapping becomes one JSON object. Use this to convert YAML-structured pipeline outputs into JSON for downstream caps that consume JSON arrays.

## Inputs

A sequence of YAML mapping files (each file is a single YAML mapping).

## Outputs

A JSON array where each element is an object derived from one input YAML mapping.

## Notes

- For YAML output, use [Merge YAML Mappings](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:record;textable;yaml";op=collect_records;out="media:list;record;textable;yaml").
- For CSV output, use [Merge YAML Mappings into CSV](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:record;textable;yaml";op=collect_records;out="media:csv;list;record;textable").

args.arg_description: YAML mapping files to merge

output.output_description: JSON array containing all YAML mappings as objects

title: Merge YAML Mappings
cap_description: Merge multiple YAML mapping files into a single YAML sequence
documentation:
## Purpose

Merges a sequence of YAML mapping files into a single YAML sequence. Each mapping becomes one entry in the output list. Use this to fan-in YAML-producing parallel processing stages into a unified YAML document.

## Inputs

A sequence of YAML mapping files (each file is a single YAML mapping).

## Outputs

A single YAML sequence containing all input mappings as elements.

## Notes

- Preserves input order.
- For JSON array output, use [Merge YAML Mappings into JSON Array](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:record;textable;yaml";op=collect_records;out="media:json;list;record;textable").

args.arg_description: YAML mapping files to merge

output.output_description: Single YAML sequence containing all input mappings

title: Convert AAC to FLAC
cap_description: Decode AAC (ADTS) and re-encode as lossless FLAC
documentation:
## Purpose

Decodes a raw AAC (ADTS-framed) file and writes it as FLAC.

## Inputs

An AAC file in ADTS framing.

## Outputs

A FLAC file.

args.arg_description: Path to the AAC file to convert

title: Convert AAC to WAV
cap_description: Decode AAC (ADTS) to uncompressed WAV (PCM)
documentation:
## Purpose

Decodes a raw AAC (ADTS-framed) file and writes it as 16-bit signed PCM WAV.

## Inputs

An AAC file in ADTS framing. AAC tracks inside MP4 containers should use the M4A converter.

## Outputs

A 16-bit signed PCM WAV file.

args.arg_description: Path to the AAC file to convert

title: Convert AIFF to FLAC
cap_description: Decode AIFF and re-encode as lossless FLAC
documentation:
## Purpose

Decodes an AIFF file and writes it as FLAC. Both are lossless; FLAC is dramatically smaller.

## Inputs

An AIFF file. AIFF-C with PCM payload is supported.

## Outputs

A FLAC file.

args.arg_description: Path to the AIFF file to convert

title: Convert AIFF to WAV
cap_description: Decode an AIFF file to uncompressed WAV (PCM)
documentation:
## Purpose

Decodes an AIFF (Audio Interchange File Format) file and writes it as 16-bit signed PCM WAV. AIFF and WAV are both PCM containers; this is a re-container.

## Inputs

An AIFF file. AIFF-C with PCM payload is supported. AIFF-C with compressed payloads is not.

## Outputs

A 16-bit signed PCM WAV file.

args.arg_description: Path to the AIFF file to convert

title: Convert FLAC to WAV
cap_description: Decode FLAC to uncompressed WAV (PCM)
documentation:
## Purpose

Decodes a FLAC file and writes it as 16-bit signed PCM WAV. Both formats are lossless; this is a re-container, useful when downstream caps require WAV.

## Inputs

A FLAC file.

## Outputs

A 16-bit signed PCM WAV file. Source bit depths above 16 are dithered down.

args.arg_description: Path to the FLAC file to convert

title: Convert M4A to FLAC
cap_description: Decode an M4A (AAC-in-MP4) file and re-encode as lossless FLAC
documentation:
## Purpose

Demuxes the audio track from an M4A container, decodes the AAC bitstream, and writes it as FLAC.

## Inputs

An M4A file. AAC and ALAC tracks are supported.

## Outputs

A FLAC file.

args.arg_description: Path to the M4A file to convert

title: Convert M4A to WAV
cap_description: Decode an M4A (AAC-in-MP4) file to uncompressed WAV (PCM)
documentation:
## Purpose

Demuxes the audio track from an M4A container and decodes the AAC (or ALAC) bitstream as 16-bit signed PCM WAV.

## Inputs

An M4A file. AAC and Apple Lossless (ALAC) audio tracks are supported.

## Outputs

A 16-bit signed PCM WAV file at the source's native sample rate and channel count.

args.arg_description: Path to the M4A file to convert

title: Convert MP3 to FLAC
cap_description: Decode MP3 and re-encode as lossless FLAC
documentation:
## Purpose

Decodes an MP3 file and writes it as FLAC. Useful for archiving the decoded MP3 without further generation loss.

## Inputs

An MP3 file.

## Outputs

A FLAC file containing the decoded PCM exactly as the MP3 decoder produced it.

## Notes

The output preserves the MP3 decoder's PCM exactly; no further lossy compression is applied.

args.arg_description: Path to the MP3 file to convert

title: Convert MP3 to WAV
cap_description: Decode MP3 to uncompressed WAV (PCM)
documentation:
## Purpose

Decodes an MP3 file and writes it as 16-bit signed PCM WAV. This is the canonical step before transcription, since [Transcribe Audio (Candle)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:candle;in="media:audio;speech;wav";ml-model;op=transcribe;out="media:transcription;textable;record") requires WAV input.

## Inputs

An MP3 file (CBR or VBR, any sample rate or channel count Symphonia supports).

## Outputs

A 16-bit signed PCM WAV file at the source's native sample rate and channel count.

## Notes

The output is bit-exact decoded PCM; no resampling is performed. If a downstream consumer requires a specific sample rate, follow with a resampling cap.

args.arg_description: Path to the MP3 file to convert

title: Convert OGG to FLAC
cap_description: Decode OGG (Vorbis) and re-encode as lossless FLAC
documentation:
## Purpose

Decodes an OGG-Vorbis file and writes it as FLAC.

## Inputs

An OGG file containing a Vorbis stream.

## Outputs

A FLAC file containing the decoded PCM.

args.arg_description: Path to the OGG file to convert

title: Convert OGG to WAV
cap_description: Decode OGG (Vorbis) to uncompressed WAV (PCM)
documentation:
## Purpose

Decodes an OGG-Vorbis file and writes it as 16-bit signed PCM WAV.

## Inputs

An OGG file containing a Vorbis stream.

## Outputs

A 16-bit signed PCM WAV file at the source's native sample rate and channel count.

## Notes

Only Vorbis streams in OGG are supported. For Opus-in-OGG, use the dedicated Opus conversion cap.

args.arg_description: Path to the OGG file to convert

title: Convert Opus to FLAC
cap_description: Decode Opus and re-encode as FLAC
documentation:
## Purpose

Decodes an Opus audio file (typically wrapped in an Ogg container) and writes the resulting PCM as a FLAC file.

## Inputs

An Opus file.

## Outputs

A FLAC file containing the decoded PCM as produced by the Opus decoder at 48 kHz.

## Notes

Opus always decodes to 48 kHz PCM internally. The FLAC output therefore reports a 48 kHz sample rate regardless of the encoder hint embedded in the OpusHead identification packet.

args.arg_description: Path to the Opus file to convert

title: Convert Opus to WAV
cap_description: Decode an Opus file to uncompressed WAV (PCM)
documentation:
## Purpose

Decodes an Opus stream (in OGG container) and writes it as 16-bit signed PCM WAV. Opus is the modern lossy codec used by WebRTC and many browser-recorded audio streams.

## Inputs

An Opus file (OGG-Opus container).

## Outputs

A 16-bit signed PCM WAV file at 48 kHz (Opus's native rate).

args.arg_description: Path to the Opus file to convert

title: Convert WAV to FLAC
cap_description: Re-encode WAV as lossless FLAC
documentation:
## Purpose

Encodes a WAV file as FLAC. Both formats are lossless; FLAC is typically half the size or less for the same audio.

## Inputs

A 16-bit signed PCM WAV file.

## Outputs

A FLAC file with the same audio content.

args.arg_description: Path to the WAV file to convert

title: Convert CSV to JSON Array of Objects
cap_description: Convert CSV data to a JSON array of objects with keys from the header row
documentation:
## Purpose

Converts a CSV file into a JSON array of objects. Each data row becomes one JSON object; the CSV header row supplies the object keys. Use this when a downstream cap expects JSON but the data source is CSV.

## Inputs

A CSV file with a header row and one or more data rows.

## Outputs

A JSON array of objects, one per CSV row.

## Notes

- Column names from the header row become JSON keys. Sanitize column names upstream if they contain characters that are problematic as JSON keys.
- For YAML output, use [Convert CSV to YAML List of Mappings](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:csv;list;record;textable";op=convert_format;out="media:list;record;textable;yaml").
- For the reverse, use [Convert JSON Array of Objects to CSV](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:json;list;record;textable";op=convert_format;out="media:csv;list;record;textable").

args.arg_description: CSV data to convert

title: Convert CSV to Text List
cap_description: Convert CSV data to a list of textable values
documentation:
## Purpose

Converts CSV data into a flat list of textable values. Use this when you need the raw cell values from a CSV as individual text items, discarding the row/column structure.

## Inputs

A CSV file.

## Outputs

A list of textable string values (cells flattened from the CSV).

## Notes

- Row and column structure is lost; the output is a flat sequence of cell values.
- If you need to preserve row structure, use [Convert CSV to JSON Array of Objects](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:csv;list;record;textable";op=convert_format;out="media:json;list;record;textable") instead.

args.arg_description: CSV data to convert

title: Convert CSV to YAML List of Mappings
cap_description: Convert CSV data to a YAML sequence of mappings with keys from the header row
documentation:
## Purpose

Converts a CSV file into a YAML sequence of mappings. Each data row becomes one YAML mapping; the CSV header row supplies the keys. Use this when a downstream cap or tool expects YAML but the data source is CSV.

## Inputs

A CSV file with a header row and one or more data rows.

## Outputs

A YAML sequence of mappings, one per CSV row.

## Notes

- For JSON output instead, use [Convert CSV to JSON Array of Objects](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:csv;list;record;textable";op=convert_format;out="media:json;list;record;textable").
- For the reverse, use [Convert YAML List of Mappings to CSV](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:list;record;textable;yaml";op=convert_format;out="media:csv;list;record;textable").

## Purpose

Decodes a BMP and writes it as a JPEG, drastically reducing file size for photographic content.

## Inputs

A BMP file.

## Outputs

A JPEG file at quality 85.

title: Convert BMP to PNG
cap_description: Re-encode a BMP image as a PNG
documentation:
## Purpose

Decodes a Windows BMP file and writes it as a PNG. BMP files are large and uncompressed; PNG is the practical replacement.

## Inputs

A BMP file. Standard 24-bit and 32-bit color depths are supported.

## Outputs

A PNG file with the same pixel content.

title: Convert GIF to JPEG
cap_description: Re-encode a GIF image as a JPEG
documentation:
## Purpose

Decodes a GIF (first frame for animated GIFs) and writes it as a JPEG.

## Inputs

A GIF file. For animated GIFs, the first frame is used.

## Outputs

A JPEG file at quality 85.

## Notes

GIF transparency is composited against white. Animation frames are dropped.

args.arg_description: Path to the GIF file to convert

title: Convert GIF to PNG
cap_description: Re-encode a GIF image as a PNG
documentation:
## Purpose

Decodes a GIF (first frame for animated GIFs) and writes it as a PNG. Useful when downstream caps require PNG input.

## Inputs

A GIF file. For animated GIFs, the first frame is used.

## Outputs

A PNG file containing the (first) frame.

## Notes

Animated GIFs collapse to their first frame. Transparency is preserved.

args.arg_description: Path to the GIF file to convert

title: Convert JPEG to PNG
cap_description: Re-encode a JPEG image as a PNG
documentation:
## Purpose

Decodes a JPEG image and re-encodes it as a lossless PNG. Use this when downstream cartridges only accept PNG (for example, [Describe Image (Candle)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:candle;in="media:image;png";ml-model;op=describe_image;out="media:image-description;textable")), or when archiving JPEGs without further compression loss.

## Inputs

A JPEG file. The decoder accepts standard JFIF and Exif-tagged JPEGs.

## Outputs

A PNG file with the same pixel content.

## Notes

The conversion is decode-then-encode. JPEG quantization artifacts are preserved exactly as a PNG cannot reverse them. PNG is larger on disk than the source JPEG for typical photographic content.

args.arg_description: Path to the JPEG file to convert

title: Convert JPEG to WebP
cap_description: Re-encode a JPEG image as a lossless WebP
documentation:
## Purpose

Decodes a JPEG and writes it as a lossless WebP. Useful for archiving JPEGs without introducing further generation loss while reducing file size relative to PNG.

## Inputs

A JPEG file.

## Outputs

A lossless WebP file with the same pixel content.

## Notes

The original JPEG quantization is preserved exactly; the WebP is lossless.

args.arg_description: Path to the JPEG file to convert

title: Convert PNG to JPEG
cap_description: Re-encode a PNG image as a JPEG
documentation:
## Purpose

Decodes a PNG and re-encodes it as a JPEG. Use when a smaller file is preferred and the loss of transparency and the introduction of compression artifacts are acceptable.

## Inputs

A PNG file. RGBA inputs have the alpha channel composited against white.

## Outputs

A JPEG file at quality 85 (default).

## Notes

JPEG does not support transparency. Alpha is composited on a white background. Quality is fixed at 85; if you need different quality, request a feature.

args.arg_description: Path to the PNG file to convert

title: Convert PNG to WebP
cap_description: Re-encode a PNG image as a lossless WebP
documentation:
## Purpose

Decodes a PNG and writes it as a lossless WebP. Lossless WebP is typically smaller than PNG for the same pixel content.

## Inputs

A PNG file (with or without alpha).

## Outputs

A lossless WebP file with the same pixel content.

## Notes

This produces lossless WebP only. Lossy WebP encoding is not exposed.

args.arg_description: Path to the PNG file to convert

title: Convert TIFF to JPEG
cap_description: Re-encode a TIFF image as a JPEG
documentation:

Decodes a TIFF (the first image in a multi-image TIFF) and writes it as a JPEG.

A TIFF file (uncompressed, LZW, PackBits or Deflate). Multi-page TIFFs use the first page.

A JPEG file at quality 85.

title: Convert TIFF to PNG
cap_description: Re-encode a TIFF image as a PNG
documentation:
## Purpose

Decodes a TIFF (the first image in a multi-image TIFF) and writes it as a PNG. Useful when downstream caps require PNG input.

## Inputs

A TIFF file. For multi-page TIFFs, only the first page is used.

## Outputs

A PNG file with the same pixel content.

## Notes

Uncompressed, LZW, PackBits and Deflate-compressed TIFFs are supported. JPEG-in-TIFF and CCITT fax compressions are not supported and will fail hard.

A JPEG file at quality 85.

title: Convert JSON Array of Objects to CSV

Converts a JSON array of objects into a CSV file. Object keys become column headers; each array element becomes one row. Use this when downstream processing or export requires CSV format.

## Inputs

A JSON array where each element is an object.

## Outputs

A CSV file with a header row derived from the union of all object keys.

## Notes

- Objects with missing keys produce empty cells in those columns.
- Column order follows the key order of the first object (or union order if keys differ across objects).
- For the reverse, use [Convert CSV to JSON Array of Objects](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:csv;list;record;textable";op=convert_format;out="media:json;list;record;textable").

A JSON array of objects.

A YAML sequence of mappings.

title: Disbind PDF Into Pages
cap_description: Extract plain text content from each PDF page
documentation:
## Purpose

Splits a PDF into its individual pages and extracts the plain text content from each page. The output is a sequence of text items, one per page. Use this as the first step in any PDF processing pipeline — text extraction, summarization, search indexing, or embedding.

## Inputs

- A PDF file (path or stdin).
- An optional index range (default: `1-`, meaning all pages). Format: `1` (single page), `1-5` (pages 1 to 5), `3-` (page 3 to end).

## Outputs

A sequence of plain text values, one item per extracted page.

## Notes

- Text extraction works on digitally-created PDFs. Scanned PDFs (image-only) will produce empty or near-empty pages. Run an OCR step first for scanned documents.
- For visual rendering of pages as images (e.g. to feed into vision models), use [Render PDF Page Images](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:pdf";op=render_page_image;out="media:image;png").
- Page numbers in the index range are 1-based.

args.arg_description: Path to the document file to process
Index Range to extract (e.g., '1-5' or '10-')

output.output_description: Plain text pages, one per page in the document

title: Get Embeddings Dimensions (Candle)
cap_description: Query the dimensionality of embeddings for a given Candle model
documentation:
## Purpose

Reports the vector size that a Candle-based embedding model produces. Use this before creating a vector index so you know how many dimensions to allocate.

## Inputs

- A model spec pointing to a HuggingFace embedding model. Defaults to `all-MiniLM-L6-v2` (384 dimensions).

## Outputs

A single integer: the number of dimensions in each embedding vector this model produces.

## Notes

- The result is read from the model's saved configuration, not from running inference, so it is fast and requires no GPU memory.
- Use at pipeline setup time to parameterize downstream vector store caps.
- See also: [Generate Vector Embeddings (Candle)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:candle;in="media:textable";ml-model;op=generate_embeddings;out="media:embedding-vector;textable;record")

args.arg_description: HuggingFace model name to query dimensions for

title: Get Embeddings Dimensions (GGUF)
cap_description: Query the dimensionality of embeddings for a given GGUF model
documentation:
## Purpose

Reports the vector size that a GGUF embedding model produces by reading `n_embd` from the model's metadata. Use this before creating a vector index so you know how many dimensions to allocate.

## Inputs

- A GGUF model spec (HuggingFace repo or local path). Defaults to `nomic-embed-text-v1.5-GGUF`.

## Outputs

A single integer: the `n_embd` value from the GGUF model header.

## Notes

- Reads model metadata only — no inference is run.
- Use at pipeline setup time to parameterize downstream vector store caps.
- See also: [Generate Vector Embeddings (GGUF)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:gguf;in="media:textable";ml-model;op=generate_embeddings;out="media:embedding-vector;textable;record")

args.arg_description: GGUF model specification to query dimensions for

output.output_description: Dimensionality of the embeddings (n_embd from model metadata)

title: Get Embeddings Dimensions (MLX)
cap_description: Query the dimensionality of embeddings for a given MLX model
documentation:
## Purpose

Reports the vector size that an MLX embedding model produces. Requires Apple Silicon. Use this before creating a vector index so you know how many dimensions to allocate.

## Inputs

- A model spec pointing to an MLX embedding model directory or HuggingFace repo. Defaults to `all-MiniLM-L6-v2-4bit`.

## Outputs

A single integer: the number of dimensions in each embedding vector this model produces.

## Notes

- Reads model configuration only — no inference is run, so it is fast.
- MLX models are quantized for efficiency on Apple Silicon; verify that the quantization level is compatible with your accuracy requirements.
- See also: [Generate Vector Embeddings (MLX)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:mlx;in="media:textable";ml-model;op=generate_embeddings;out="media:embedding-vector;textable;record")

args.arg_description: Path to MLX embedding model directory

title: Generate Vector Embeddings (Candle)
cap_description: Generate vector embeddings from text using Candle ML framework on CPU/GPU
documentation:
## Purpose

Encodes text into dense floating-point vectors suitable for semantic search, clustering, classification, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). Uses sentence-transformers models running locally via Candle on CPU or GPU. Long documents are automatically split into overlapping chunks; each chunk gets its own vector.

## Inputs

- A text file to embed.
- A model spec (HuggingFace repo). Defaults to `all-MiniLM-L6-v2` (384 dimensions, general-purpose).
- Chunk size in words (default 400) and chunk overlap in words (default 50). Adjust to match your retrieval granularity.

## Outputs

A JSON record containing an array of embedding objects. Each object includes the chunk text, its position in the document, the embedding vector (array of floats), and model metadata (model name, dimensions).

## Notes

- Choose chunk size based on context: smaller chunks (100-200 words) give precise retrieval; larger chunks (500-800 words) give more context per match.
- Overlap prevents important content from being cut across chunk boundaries.
- Candle supports both CPU and CUDA GPU. GPU significantly reduces latency for large documents.
- Use [Get Embeddings Dimensions (Candle)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:candle;in="media:model-spec;candle;textable;embeddings";ml-model;op=embeddings_dimensions;out="media:model-dim;integer;textable;numeric") to check the vector size before building an index.
- For GGUF-quantized models, see [Generate Vector Embeddings (GGUF)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:gguf;in="media:textable";ml-model;op=generate_embeddings;out="media:embedding-vector;textable;record").

args.arg_description: Path to text file to generate embeddings from
HuggingFace model name for embeddings
Chunk size in words for splitting input text
Chunk overlap in words when splitting input text

output.output_description: JSON containing embeddings, chunk metadata and model information

title: Generate Vector Embeddings (GGUF)
cap_description: Generate vector embeddings from text using GGUF models with llama.cpp backend
documentation:
## Purpose

Encodes text into dense floating-point vectors using quantized GGUF embedding models via llama.cpp. Suitable for semantic search, clustering, and RAG. Long documents are automatically split into overlapping chunks.

## Inputs

- A GGUF model spec (HuggingFace repo or local path). Defaults to `nomic-embed-text-v1.5-GGUF`.
- A text file to embed.
- Chunk size in words (default 400), chunk overlap in words (default 50), and batch size in tokens (default 2048).

## Outputs

A JSON record containing an array of embedding objects. Each object includes the chunk text, its position, the L2-normalized embedding vector, and model metadata.

## Notes

- GGUF quantization makes models smaller and faster at a small accuracy trade-off. Q4 and Q8 quantizations are common choices.
- Vectors are L2-normalized. Use cosine similarity or dot product when comparing vectors from this cap.
- Batch size must be large enough to accommodate the tokenized chunk. Increase if you see truncation errors.
- Use [Get Embeddings Dimensions (GGUF)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:gguf;in="media:model-spec;gguf;textable;embeddings";ml-model;op=embeddings_dimensions;out="media:model-dim;integer;textable;numeric") to check the vector size.
- For Apple Silicon, prefer [Generate Vector Embeddings (MLX)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:mlx;in="media:textable";ml-model;op=generate_embeddings;out="media:embedding-vector;textable;record").

args.arg_description: GGUF model specification (HuggingFace repo or local path)
Path to text file to generate embeddings from
Chunk size in words for splitting input text
Chunk overlap in words when splitting input text
Token batch size for model inference (must accommodate tokenized chunk)

output.output_description: JSON containing L2-normalized embeddings, chunk metadata and model information

title: Generate Image Embeddings (Candle)
cap_description: Generate image embeddings using CLIP models with Candle ML framework
documentation:
## Purpose

Encodes a PNG image into a dense floating-point vector using CLIP (Contrastive Language-Image Pretraining). The resulting vector lives in the same semantic space as CLIP text embeddings, enabling cross-modal similarity search — find images by text queries and vice versa.

## Inputs

- A PNG image file.
- A CLIP model spec from HuggingFace. Defaults to `clip-vit-base-patch32` (512 dimensions).

## Outputs

A JSON record containing the image embedding vector and model metadata.

## Notes

- CLIP image and text vectors are compatible: you can compare an image embedding to text embeddings generated by a CLIP text encoder in the same vector space.
- Input must be PNG. Convert other image formats upstream.
- For describing image content in natural language rather than embedding it, use [Describe Image (Candle)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:candle;in="media:image;png";ml-model;op=describe_image;out="media:image-description;textable").

args.arg_description: Path to the image file to embed
CLIP model name from HuggingFace

output.output_description: Image embedding vector with model metadata

title: Generate Vector Embeddings (MLX)
cap_description: Generate vector embeddings from text using MLX-based models on Apple Silicon
documentation:
## Purpose

Encodes text into dense floating-point vectors using MLX-optimized embedding models on Apple Silicon. Delivers fast, energy-efficient embedding on Mac M-series hardware. Long documents are automatically chunked.

## Inputs

- A model spec (HuggingFace repo or local path). Defaults to `all-MiniLM-L6-v2-4bit` (384 dimensions, 4-bit quantized).
- A text file to embed.
- Chunk size in words (default 400) and chunk overlap in words (default 50).

## Outputs

A JSON record containing an array of embedding objects with chunk text, position, embedding vector, and model metadata.

## Notes

- Requires Apple Silicon (M1 or later). Not available on Intel Macs or non-Apple hardware.
- MLX uses the unified memory architecture; large models share CPU/GPU memory without copying.
- Use [Get Embeddings Dimensions (MLX)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:model-spec;mlx;textable;embeddings";ml-model;mlx;op=embeddings_dimensions;out="media:model-dim;integer;textable;numeric") to check the vector size.
- For cross-platform pipelines, use [Generate Vector Embeddings (Candle)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:candle;in="media:textable";ml-model;op=generate_embeddings;out="media:embedding-vector;textable;record") or [Generate Vector Embeddings (GGUF)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:gguf;in="media:textable";ml-model;op=generate_embeddings;out="media:embedding-vector;textable;record") instead.

args.arg_description: Path to MLX embedding model directory
Path to text file to generate embeddings from
Chunk size in words for splitting input text
Chunk overlap in words when splitting input text

output.output_description: JSON containing embeddings, chunk metadata and model information

title: Extract Audio Track from MKV
cap_description: Demux the audio track from a Matroska MKV and write it as an Opus file
documentation:
## Purpose

Extracts the audio track from a Matroska MKV container and writes it as an Opus file.

## Inputs

An MKV file with an Opus audio track.

## Outputs

An Opus file (OGG-Opus). No re-encoding occurs.

## Notes

If the MKV has any audio codec other than Opus, this cap fails hard. Other codec adapters can be added as separate caps.

args.arg_description: Path to the MKV file

title: Extract Audio Track from MOV
cap_description: Demux the audio track from a QuickTime MOV and write it as an M4A file
documentation:
## Purpose

Extracts the AAC audio track from a QuickTime MOV file and writes it as an M4A. The MOV container is structurally identical to MP4 for the audio remux path.

## Inputs

A MOV file with an AAC audio track.

## Outputs

An M4A file containing the source's AAC audio track. No re-encoding occurs.

## Notes

If the MOV has no AAC audio track, the cap fails hard. There is no audio re-encoding fallback.

args.arg_description: Path to the MOV file

title: Extract Audio Track from MP4
cap_description: Demux the audio track from an MP4 video and write it as an M4A file
documentation:
## Purpose

Extracts the AAC audio track from an MP4 container and writes it as an M4A file (the same AAC bitstream, re-containered without re-encoding). This is the canonical first step when you have a video and want to feed its audio into a transcription pipeline.

## Inputs

An MP4 file with an AAC audio track. Video tracks are ignored.

## Outputs

An M4A file containing the source's AAC audio track. No re-encoding occurs; the AAC bitstream is bit-exact.

## Notes

If the MP4 has no AAC audio track, the cap fails hard. There is no audio re-encoding fallback.

args.arg_description: Path to the MP4 file

title: Extract Audio Track from WebM
cap_description: Demux the audio track from a WebM container and write it as an Opus file
documentation:
## Purpose

Extracts the audio track from a WebM container and writes it as an Opus file (OGG-Opus). WebM uses Opus or Vorbis for audio; this cap targets Opus.

## Inputs

A WebM file with an Opus audio track.

## Outputs

An Opus file (OGG-Opus). No re-encoding occurs.

## Notes

If the WebM has a Vorbis audio track instead of Opus, this cap fails hard. There is no codec fallback.

args.arg_description: Path to the WebM file

title: Generate JSON
cap_description: Generate JSON from text content, constrained to validate against a caller-supplied JSON schema.
documentation:
## Purpose

Runs constrained LLM generation over the supplied content to produce a JSON
object that validates against the supplied JSON schema. Token-level schema
enforcement (via LLGuidance on the GGUF backend) guarantees the output parses
and satisfies the schema — downstream code can `serde_json::from_str::<T>` or
its equivalent without defensive error handling.

## Inputs

- Content to analyze — plain text (the cap's stdin-flowing input).
- `--schema`: a JSON Schema object describing the required output shape.
- Language code (default: `en`).
- A model spec (defaults to Mistral-7B-Instruct Q8_0 GGUF).

## Outputs

A JSON object conforming to the supplied schema.

## Notes

- The output schema is supplied per-call, not looked up from any registry.
  Callers who want a fixed-shape "named query" endpoint should define their
  own cap that wraps this one with a baked-in schema.
- Constraint enforcement is at the token-sampling level — the model cannot
  emit tokens that would violate the schema.
- See also: [Make a Decision](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:constrained;in="media:textable";language=en;op=make_decision;out="media:decision;json;record;textable")
  and [Make Multiple Decisions](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:constrained;in="media:textable";language=en;op=make_multiple_decisions;out="media:decision;json;record;textable")
  for fixed-shape boolean classification caps.

args.arg_description: Text content for the model to analyze.
JSON Schema the generated output must validate against.
Language code for query processing.
Model spec to use for constrained generation.

output.output_description: JSON object conforming to the supplied schema.

title: Generate Code in English
cap_description: Code generation and programming tasks in English
documentation:
## Purpose

Generates source code from a natural-language prompt using a locally running LLM. Supports specifying the target programming language, optional existing code context, style preferences, and whether to include unit tests and comments.

## Inputs

- A text prompt describing what code to write.
- Programming language (default: Rust).
- Optional code context (existing code or codebase description).
- Optional coding style or framework preferences.
- Max tokens (default 4096 — higher than other tasks to accommodate complete code).
- Flags for including unit tests and comments.
- A model spec (defaults to Mistral-7B-Instruct Q8_0).

## Outputs

A JSON record with the generated code, detected language, and metadata (token count, model used).

## Notes

- This cap uses constrained LLM generation to improve structural reliability of the output.
- Increase max tokens if the generated code is being truncated.
- For general text generation tasks, use [Summarize in English](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:constrained;in="media:textable";language=en;op=summarization;out="media:generated-text;textable;record") or the raw inference caps instead.

args.arg_description: Code generation prompt or specification
Programming language for code generation
Existing code context or codebase information
Coding style or framework preferences
Maximum tokens for generated code
Whether to include unit tests
Whether to include code comments
Model spec to use for code generation

output.output_description: Generated code with optional tests and documentation

title: Write Creatively in English
cap_description: Creative writing and content generation in English
documentation:
## Purpose

Generates creative written content (fiction, poetry, marketing copy, blog posts, scripts, etc.) from a prompt using a locally running LLM. Genre, tone, length, and creativity level are all tunable.

## Inputs

- A creative writing prompt or brief.
- Genre (default: fiction). Examples: `fiction`, `poetry`, `blog`, `marketing`, `script`.
- Tone (default: conversational). Examples: `formal`, `humorous`, `dramatic`, `inspirational`.
- Output length (default: medium). Values: `short`, `medium`, `long`.
- Optional background context or inspiration material.
- Max tokens (default 3000).
- Creativity level 0.0-1.0 (default 0.8 — maps to sampling temperature).

## Outputs

A JSON record with the generated creative content and metadata.

## Notes

- Higher creativity level increases output variety and imagination at the cost of coherence. Values above 0.9 can produce surprising but sometimes incoherent output.
- Provide detailed context to anchor the model on your specific creative direction.

args.arg_description: Creative writing prompt or brief
Genre or style of creative content
Tone or mood for the creative content
Desired length of creative output
Background context or inspiration
Maximum tokens for creative output
Creativity level (0.0-1.0, higher = more creative)
Model spec to use for creative writing

title: Answer Multiple Choice in English
cap_description: Logical reasoning and binary decision tasks in English
documentation:
## Purpose

Analyzes text and performs logical reasoning to answer a multiple-choice or binary decision question. Returns the decision along with a reasoning explanation and confidence score. Useful for classification, routing, and document analysis pipelines.

## Inputs

- A text prompt containing the question or decision problem.
- Optional reasoning context (facts, background information).
- Decision type (default: `binary`). Use `binary` for yes/no decisions; other values for multi-class reasoning.
- Max tokens (default 1000).
- A flag to include reasoning explanation in the output (default true).

## Outputs

A JSON record with the decision, reasoning explanation, and confidence score.

## Notes

- For strict binary classification with a guaranteed true/false output and no explanation overhead, use [Make a Decision](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:constrained;in="media:textable";language=en;op=make_decision;out="media:decision;json;record;textable") instead.
- Include context for better accuracy on domain-specific questions.

args.arg_description: Logical query or decision problem
Context and facts for logical reasoning
Type of logical decision required
Maximum tokens for reasoning output
Whether to include reasoning explanation
Model spec to use for decision making

output.output_description: Logical decision with reasoning explanation and confidence

title: Summarize in English
cap_description: Document and text summarization in English
documentation:
## Purpose

Summarizes text documents or passages using a locally running LLM. Supports abstractive (paraphrased) and extractive summarization, with controls for length, focus area, and document structure preservation.

## Inputs

- The text content to summarize.
- Summary type (default: `abstractive`). Use `extractive` to surface sentences from the original.
- Output length (default: `medium`). Values: `short`, `medium`, `long`.
- Focus area (default: `main_points`). Examples: `conclusions`, `action_items`, `key_facts`.
- Optional additional context for domain-specific summarization.
- Max tokens (default 1000).
- Preserve structure flag: when true, the summary mirrors the section structure of the input.

## Outputs

A JSON record with the summary text and metadata including compression ratio and model information.

## Notes

- Feed this cap the output of [Disbind PDF Into Pages](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:pdf";op=disbind;out="media:textable;page") to summarize PDFs page by page.
- For very long documents, chunk them upstream and summarize each chunk, then summarize the summaries.

args.arg_description: Text content to summarize
Type of summary to generate
Desired summary length
Summary focus area
Additional context for summarization
Maximum tokens for summary output
Whether to preserve document structure
Model spec to use for summarization

output.output_description: Summary with metadata including compression metrics

title: List Compatible Models
cap_description: List locally cached models whose architecture (config.json model_type) is in the supplied set.
documentation:
## Purpose

Returns the subset of cached models whose detected architecture matches one of the requested architectures. Drives cap-aware model pickers: a cap declares the architectures it can run via `supported_model_types`, the picker forwards that set here, and the user is presented only with usable models.

## Inputs

A JSON record:

```
{ "architectures": ["mistral", "llama", "qwen2"] }
```

## Outputs

A JSON record listing matching models:

```
{
  "architectures": ["mistral", "llama", "qwen2"],
  "models": [
    {
      "model_spec": "hf:org/model",
      "local_path": "/path/to/model",
      "model_type": "mistral"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Notes

- The architecture is read from each cached model's `config.json` `model_type` field. Models without a parseable `config.json` are excluded — a cap that does not recognise an arch cannot load that model.
- The index is in-memory and rebuilt lazily from the cache directory on the first call after process start. Subsequent calls are cheap.

args.arg_description: JSON record { "architectures": ["mistral", "llama", ...] } — the architecture set the calling cap supports.

output.output_description: JSON record listing matching cached models with local paths and model_type.

title: LLM Text Generation (Candle)
cap_description: Generate text using Candle transformer models. Output is NDJSON streaming tokens.
documentation:
## Purpose

Runs autoregressive text generation using Candle transformer models (safetensors format). Accepts a fully-structured generation request and streams output as NDJSON tokens. This is the low-level inference cap used by higher-level caps like Summarize and Generate Code.

## Inputs

A single JSON generation request record containing: the prompt, model spec (HuggingFace safetensors repo), sampling parameters (temperature, top-p, max tokens, seed), and an optional system prompt.

Optional HuggingFace bearer token (`hf_token`) for gated repositories — required for `gemma`, `gemma2`, `gemma3` and any other licence-protected model.

## Outputs

An NDJSON stream. Each line is a JSON object. Lines carry token text, status events (`started`, `complete`), and a final summary with total tokens and timing.

## Notes

- Every execution is streaming — there is no non-streaming mode.
- Candle runs on CPU, Metal, or CUDA. GPU is strongly recommended for models larger than 3B parameters.
- Architectures supported: mistral, llama, qwen2, qwen3, phi, phi3, gemma, gemma2, gemma3, falcon, olmo, olmo2. The architecture is detected from the model's `config.json` `model_type` — pickers driven by `supported_model_types` will reject any model whose architecture is outside this list. Yi is unsupported in this cap because `candle-transformers` 0.9.2 ships a non-deserialisable `yi::Config`; use the GGUF or MLX backends for Yi.
- For higher-level tasks (summarization, code generation, creative writing), use the task-specific inference caps rather than this one directly.
- For constrained output (JSON schema, regex), use [Constrained LLM Generation (GGUF)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:constrained;gguf;in="media:llm-generation-request;json;record";llm;ml-model;op=llm_inference_constrained;out="media:llm-text-stream;ndjson").

args.arg_description: The generation request containing prompt, model spec, and parameters
HuggingFace bearer token. Required when targeting a gated repository (license-protected models). Without it, gated downloads fail with a clear authentication error.

output.output_description: NDJSON stream of tokens, status updates, and completion message

title: Constrained LLM Generation (GGUF)
cap_description: Generate text with LLGuidance constraints (JSON schema, regex, grammar). Output is NDJSON streaming.
documentation:
## Purpose

Runs autoregressive text generation with hard structural constraints using LLGuidance. The model is guaranteed to produce output that matches the supplied JSON schema, regular expression, or grammar — making this the right cap when downstream code must parse the LLM output without error handling for malformed text.

## Inputs

A JSON generation request record that additionally includes a `constraint` field. The constraint can be one of:
- `json_schema`: a JSON Schema object the output must validate against
- `regex`: a regular expression string the output must match
- `grammar`: a context-free grammar in GBNF or LLGuidance format

## Outputs

An NDJSON stream of constrained tokens and a final completion message, same format as the unconstrained inference caps.

## Notes

- Constraint enforcement happens at the token-sampling level via LLGuidance — the model cannot produce tokens that would violate the constraint.
- Structured output via JSON schema is the most common use case: specify the exact fields and types you need and receive valid JSON every time.
- Performance overhead compared to unconstrained inference is small for simple schemas; complex grammars may add latency.
- Use this cap when building pipelines where a downstream cap expects well-formed JSON and you cannot tolerate parse failures.
- See also: [Make a Decision](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:constrained;in="media:textable";language=en;op=make_decision;out="media:decision;json;record;textable") and [Generate JSON](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:constrained;in="media:textable";language=en;op=generate_json;out="media:json;textable;record") for higher-level constrained tasks.

args.arg_description: The generation request with constraint field (json_schema, regex, or grammar)
HuggingFace bearer token. Required when targeting a gated repository.

output.output_description: NDJSON stream of constrained tokens and completion

title: LLM Text Generation (GGUF)
cap_description: Generate text using GGUF models with llama.cpp backend. Output is NDJSON streaming tokens.
documentation:
## Purpose

Runs autoregressive text generation using quantized GGUF models via llama.cpp. Accepts a fully-structured generation request and streams output as NDJSON tokens. This is the primary low-level inference cap for GGUF-based pipelines.

## Inputs

A single JSON generation request record containing: the prompt, model spec (HuggingFace GGUF repo or local path), sampling parameters (temperature, top-k, top-p, min-p, repeat penalty, max tokens, seed, context length), and an optional system prompt.

Optional HuggingFace bearer token (`hf_token`) for gated repositories.

## Outputs

An NDJSON stream. Each line is a JSON object carrying token text, status events (`started`, `complete`), and a final summary with token counts and timing.

## Notes

- Every execution is streaming — there is no non-streaming mode.
- GGUF quantization reduces memory requirements significantly. Q8_0 preserves most accuracy; Q4_K_M is a good balance of size and quality.
- For constrained output (JSON schema, regex), use [Constrained LLM Generation (GGUF)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:constrained;gguf;in="media:llm-generation-request;json;record";llm;ml-model;op=llm_inference_constrained;out="media:llm-text-stream;ndjson").
- For Apple Silicon, prefer [LLM Text Generation (MLX)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:llm-generation-request;json;record";llm;ml-model;mlx;op=llm_inference;out="media:llm-text-stream;ndjson").

args.arg_description: The generation request containing prompt, model spec, and parameters
HuggingFace bearer token. Required when targeting a gated repository.

output.output_description: NDJSON stream of tokens, status updates, and completion message

title: LLM Text Generation (MLX)
cap_description: Generate text using MLX models on Apple Silicon. Output is NDJSON streaming tokens.
documentation:
## Purpose

Runs autoregressive text generation using MLX-optimized models on Apple Silicon. Accepts a fully-structured generation request and streams output as NDJSON tokens. Designed for fast, energy-efficient inference on Mac M-series hardware.

## Inputs

A single JSON generation request record containing: the prompt, model spec (MLX HuggingFace repo or local path), sampling parameters (temperature, top-p, repeat penalty, max tokens, seed), and an optional system prompt.

## Outputs

An NDJSON stream. Each line is a JSON object carrying token text, status events (`started`, `complete`), and a final summary with token counts and timing.

## Notes

- Requires Apple Silicon (M1 or later).
- MLX uses unified memory — models share CPU/GPU memory without copying, giving practical access to large models on machines with ample RAM.
- Every execution is streaming.
- For cross-platform pipelines, use [LLM Text Generation (GGUF)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:gguf;in="media:llm-generation-request;json;record";llm;ml-model;op=llm_inference;out="media:llm-text-stream;ndjson").

args.arg_description: The generation request containing prompt, model spec, and parameters
HuggingFace bearer token. Required when targeting a gated repository.

output.output_description: NDJSON stream of tokens, status updates, and completion message

title: Get Model Info (GGUF)
cap_description: Query metadata and capabilities of a GGUF model
documentation:
## Purpose

Returns metadata stored in a GGUF model file's header: architecture, parameter count, quantization type, context length, vocabulary size, and other configuration fields. Use this to inspect a model before using it in a pipeline.

## Inputs

A generation request JSON record with `request_type` set to `model_info` and the model spec to inspect.

## Outputs

A JSON object with model metadata fields extracted from the GGUF header.

## Notes

- No inference is run; this reads the GGUF file metadata only.
- Use this cap to verify that a model's context length and architecture are compatible with your pipeline before committing to a full inference run.
- For vocabulary extraction (needed by constrained generation), use [Get Model Vocabulary (GGUF)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:gguf;in="media:llm-generation-request;json;record";llm;ml-model;op=llm_vocab;out="media:llm-vocab-response;json;record").

args.arg_description: Request with request_type=model_info and model_spec

title: Generate Text with LLM (Candle)
cap_description: Generate text using Candle transformer models (multi-architecture safetensors).
documentation:
## Purpose

Accepts a raw text prompt and generates a text continuation using a Candle safetensors LLM. This is a simpler, prompt-in / text-out interface compared to the structured request used by the llm-inference caps. Suitable for quick text generation without building a full generation request object.

## Inputs

- A text prompt (plain text, passed via stdin or positional arg).
- A model spec (HuggingFace safetensors repo). The default is configurable.
- Sampling parameters: max tokens (512), temperature (0.7), top-p (0.9), seed (42).
- Optional HuggingFace bearer token (`hf_token`) for gated repositories — required for `gemma`, `gemma2`, `gemma3` and any other licence-protected model.

## Outputs

Generated text streamed as raw text chunks (not NDJSON).

## Notes

- Architectures supported: mistral, llama, qwen2, qwen3, phi, phi3, gemma, gemma2, gemma3, falcon, olmo, olmo2. The architecture is detected from the model's `config.json` `model_type` — pickers driven by `supported_model_types` will reject any model whose architecture is outside this list. Yi is unsupported in this cap because `candle-transformers` 0.9.2 ships a non-deserialisable `yi::Config`; use the GGUF or MLX backends for Yi.
- For structured output, system prompts, or streaming JSON events, use [LLM Text Generation (Candle)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:candle;in="media:llm-generation-request;json;record";llm;ml-model;op=llm_inference;out="media:llm-text-stream;ndjson") instead.
- Requires safetensors model weights (not GGUF).
- Gated repositories require `hf_token`. Without one, the call fails hard with a clear authentication error rather than silently downgrading.

args.arg_description: Input text prompt for generation
Candle model specification (HuggingFace repo with safetensors)
HuggingFace bearer token. Required when targeting a gated repository (license-protected models). Without it, gated downloads fail with a clear authentication error.
Maximum number of tokens to generate
Sampling temperature (0.0-2.0)
Top-p (nucleus) sampling parameter
Random seed for reproducibility

output.output_description: Generated text streamed as raw text chunks

title: Generate Text with LLM (GGUF)

## Notes

- For structured output, system prompts, or streaming JSON events, use [LLM Text Generation (GGUF)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:gguf;in="media:llm-generation-request;json;record";llm;ml-model;op=llm_inference;out="media:llm-text-stream;ndjson") instead.
- Increase context length and batch size for long-form generation with large prompts.
