output.output_description: Generated text streamed as raw text chunks

title: Get Model Vocabulary (GGUF)
cap_description: Extract vocabulary tokens from a GGUF model for constraint initialization
documentation:
## Purpose

Extracts the full token vocabulary from a GGUF model. The vocabulary is required by LLGuidance and other constrained decoding backends before they can compile JSON schemas, regexes, or grammars into token-level masks.

## Inputs

A generation request JSON record with `request_type` set to `vocab` and the model spec to read.

## Outputs

A JSON object with a `vocab` array (list of token strings) and a `vocab_size` integer.

## Notes

- This cap is typically called once per model at constraint initialization time, not per inference request.
- The vocab array indices correspond to token IDs used internally by the model's tokenizer.
- See also: [Constrained LLM Generation (GGUF)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:constrained;gguf;in="media:llm-generation-request;json;record";llm;ml-model;op=llm_inference_constrained;out="media:llm-text-stream;ndjson") which depends on this vocabulary.

args.arg_description: Request with request_type=vocab and model_spec

output.output_description: JSON object with vocab array and vocab_size

title: Make a Decision
cap_description: Make a single binary (yes/no, true/false) decision based on content and a question
documentation:
## Purpose

Classifies content against a single yes/no question using constrained LLM generation, guaranteeing a structured JSON boolean output. Use this for routing, filtering, and classification steps in pipelines where downstream code expects a machine-readable true/false value.

## Inputs

- Content to analyze (text).
- A binary question about that content (e.g. "Does this document contain personally identifiable information?").
- Language code (default: `en`).
- Max content length (default 2048 characters — content is truncated to fit the context window).
- Confidence threshold (default 0.7) — decisions below this threshold may be flagged.
- A model spec (defaults to Mistral-7B-Instruct Q8_0).

## Outputs

A JSON record with a `value` boolean field and a confidence score.

## Notes

- Output is guaranteed to be valid JSON (via LLGuidance constraint) — no parsing of free-form text required.
- For multiple simultaneous questions against the same content, use [Make Multiple Decisions](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:constrained;in="media:textable";language=en;op=make_multiple_decisions;out="media:decision;json;record;textable") to avoid redundant model loads.
- For decisions with natural-language explanations, use [Answer Multiple Choice in English](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:constrained;in="media:textable";language=en;op=multiplechoice;out="media:generated-text;textable;record").

args.arg_description: Content to analyze for the binary decision
The binary question to answer about the content
Language code for processing
Maximum content length to consider (for context window management)
Minimum confidence threshold for decisions
Model spec to use for decision making

title: Make Multiple Decisions
cap_description: Make multiple binary (yes/no, true/false) decisions based on content and multiple questions
documentation:
## Purpose

Classifies content against multiple yes/no questions in a single model call using constrained LLM generation. More efficient than calling [Make a Decision](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:constrained;in="media:textable";language=en;op=make_decision;out="media:decision;json;record;textable") repeatedly when you have several questions about the same document.

## Inputs

- Content to analyze (text).
- A sequence of binary questions (one per line, passed as a multi-value argument). All questions are evaluated against the same content in one inference pass.
- Language code (default: `en`).
- Max content length (default 2048).
- Confidence threshold (default 0.7).
- A model spec (defaults to Mistral-7B-Instruct Q8_0).

## Outputs

A sequence of JSON records, one per question, each containing a question identifier and a boolean `value`.

## Notes

- Output order matches the input question order.
- All questions share the same content context in a single inference call, which is significantly more efficient than one call per question.
- Output is guaranteed structured JSON via LLGuidance constraint.

args.arg_description: Content to analyze for the binary decisions
Binary questions to answer about the content (one per line)
Language code for processing
Maximum content length to consider (for context window management)
Minimum confidence threshold for decisions
Model spec to use for decision making

output.output_description: One decision per question, each as a JSON object with identifier and value

title: Check Model Availability
cap_description: Check if a model is available locally and/or remotely.
documentation:
## Purpose

Checks whether a model is present in the local cache and/or accessible from its remote repository. Use this before a pipeline that depends on a model to give users a clear message if the model needs to be downloaded first.

## Inputs

A model spec string (e.g. `hf:org/model-name`).

## Outputs

A JSON record with local availability status, remote reachability, local cache path (if present), and file size information.

## Notes

- Does not download the model. Use [Download Model](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:model-spec;textable";op=download-model;out="media:download-result;textable;record") to fetch it.
- For download progress tracking on an already-initiated download, use [Model Status](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:model-spec;textable";op=model-status;out="media:model-status;textable;record").

args.arg_description: Model spec to check availability for

output.output_description: Model availability information including local and remote status

title: Model Contents
cap_description: List contents of a model repository.
documentation:
## Purpose

Lists the files inside a model repository along with their sizes, types, and metadata. Useful for inspecting what shards, config files, and tokenizer assets a model contains before deciding which files to download.

## Inputs

A model spec string (e.g. `hf:org/model-name`).

## Outputs

A JSON record with the list of repository files, their sizes, and metadata.

## Notes

- This lists the remote repository contents; it does not require the model to be downloaded locally.
- Use this when building a model spec with `include`/`exclude` filters to understand which files are available to filter on.
- See also: [Check Model Availability](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:model-spec;textable";op=model-availability;out="media:model-availability;textable;record") and [Download Model](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:model-spec;textable";op=download-model;out="media:download-result;textable;record").

args.arg_description: Model spec to list contents for

output.output_description: Model contents including files, layers, parameters and metadata

title: Download Model
cap_description: Download a model from repository to local cache.
documentation:
## Purpose

Downloads a model from a remote repository (e.g. HuggingFace) to the local model cache. Subsequent inference caps will use the cached copy without network access.

## Inputs

A model spec string (e.g. `hf:org/model-name` or `hf:org/model-name?include=*.gguf`). Include/exclude filters in the spec control which files are downloaded.

## Outputs

A JSON record with the local cache path, downloaded file list, and download metadata.

## Notes

- Downloads are resumable. If interrupted, re-running the cap continues from where it stopped.
- Use `include`/`exclude` filters in the model spec to download only the quantization variant you need rather than the full repository.
- Check progress of an ongoing download with [Model Status](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:model-spec;textable";op=model-status;out="media:model-status;textable;record").
- After downloading, confirm the local path with [Get Model Path](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:model-spec;textable";op=model-path;out="media:model-path;textable;record").

args.arg_description: Model spec (e.g., hf:org/model-name)

HuggingFace bearer token. Required when downloading a gated repository (license-protected models). Without it, gated downloads fail with a clear authentication error rather than silently falling back to anonymous access.

output.output_description: Download result with model path and metadata

title: List Models
cap_description: List all locally cached models.
documentation:
## Purpose

Returns a list of all models currently in the local model cache, along with their sizes, download status, and metadata. Use this to see what is already available on the current machine.

## Inputs

An optional model repository identifier (defaults to `huggingface`).

## Outputs

A JSON record with a list of cached models, each including the model spec, local path, file sizes, and last-accessed time.

## Notes

- Only shows locally cached models, not all models available remotely.
- Use [Check Model Availability](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:model-spec;textable";op=model-availability;out="media:model-availability;textable;record") to check a specific model.
- Use [Download Model](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:model-spec;textable";op=download-model;out="media:download-result;textable;record") to add a model to the local cache.

args.arg_description: Model repository to list from

output.output_description: List of models with their metadata and status

title: Get Model Path
cap_description: Get local filesystem path to a cached model.
documentation:
## Purpose

Returns the absolute filesystem path to a model that is already in the local cache. Use this when a downstream tool or script needs to reference the model by path rather than by spec.

## Inputs

A model spec string (e.g. `hf:org/model-name`).

## Outputs

A JSON record with the local directory path to the model and a list of files inside it.

## Notes

- Fails if the model is not in the local cache. Download it first with [Download Model](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:model-spec;textable";op=download-model;out="media:download-result;textable;record").
- Useful for integrating with tools that require a filesystem path (e.g. llama.cpp CLI, custom scripts).

args.arg_description: Model spec to get path for

output.output_description: Local filesystem path to the model directory

title: Model Status
cap_description: Get download status for a model.
documentation:
## Purpose

Returns the current download status and health of a model. Use this to poll progress during an active download, or to verify that a previously downloaded model is intact.

## Inputs

A model spec string (e.g. `hf:org/model-name`).

## Outputs

A JSON record including download state (not started, in progress, complete, error), progress percentage, health checks, and any error messages.

## Notes

- Poll this cap periodically during a long download to show progress to the user.
- Also reports whether the local cached copy passes integrity checks.
- See also: [Check Model Availability](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:model-spec;textable";op=model-availability;out="media:model-availability;textable;record") for a simpler present/absent check.

args.arg_description: Model spec to check status for

output.output_description: Model status including health, progress and configuration

title: Render Log Page Image
cap_description: Render a page-like PNG image preview of the log document
documentation:
## Purpose

Renders a log file as a page-like PNG image. Useful for generating visual snapshots of log output for display in dashboards, reports, or image-aware pipeline steps.

## Inputs

- A log file (path or stdin, media type `media:log;textable`).
- Page width in pixels (default 800).
- Page height in pixels (default 1200).

## Outputs

A PNG image representing the rendered log page.

## Notes

- For plain text files that are not log files, use [Render Text Page Image](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:txt;textable";op=render_page_image;out="media:image;png").
- Long log files will overflow the page. Tail the log upstream if you only need recent entries.

args.arg_description: Path to the log file to process
Width of the page image in pixels
Height of the page image in pixels

output.output_description: PNG image data representing the rendered log page

title: Render Markdown Page Image
cap_description: Render a page-like PNG image preview of the markdown document
documentation:
## Purpose

Renders a Markdown file as a page-like PNG image. The Markdown is formatted and laid out as it would appear in a reader, then captured as a PNG. Useful for generating document previews and thumbnails of Markdown content.

## Inputs

- A Markdown file (path or stdin).
- Page width in pixels (default 800).
- Page height in pixels (default 1200).

## Outputs

A PNG image representing the rendered Markdown page.

## Notes

- Unlike the PDF renderer, this produces a single image (not a sequence) because Markdown files are treated as single-page documents.
- For ingesting Markdown text into LLM or embedding pipelines, read the raw `.md` file directly rather than rendering it to an image first.

args.arg_description: Path to the markdown file to process
Width of the page image in pixels
Height of the page image in pixels

output.output_description: PNG image data representing the rendered markdown page

title: Render PDF Page Images
cap_description: Render PNG page images for one or more PDF pages
documentation:
## Purpose

Renders one or more pages of a PDF as PNG images. Each page becomes a separate PNG output. Use this to feed PDF content into vision models, generate thumbnails, or produce visual previews.

## Inputs

- A PDF file (path or stdin).
- Page image width in pixels (default 800).
- Page image height in pixels (default 1200).
- An optional index range (default: `1-`, all pages). Format: `1` (single page), `1-5` (pages 1-5), `3-` (page 3 to end). Ranges are 1-based.

## Outputs

A sequence of PNG images, one per rendered page.

## Notes

- Higher resolution (larger width/height) produces more readable images at the cost of larger file sizes.
- Feed the PNG output into [Describe Image (MLX)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:image;png";ml-model;mlx;op=describe_image;out="media:image-description;textable";vision) or [Generate Image Embeddings (Candle)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:candle;in="media:image;png";ml-model;op=generate_image_embeddings;out="media:embedding-vector;textable;record") for vision-based PDF analysis.
- For plain text extraction, use [Disbind PDF Into Pages](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:pdf";op=disbind;out="media:textable;page") instead.

args.arg_description: Path to the document file to process
Width of the page image in pixels
Height of the page image in pixels
Index Range to render from (1-based). Examples: '1' (single page), '1-5' (pages 1-5), '3-' (page 3 to end)

output.output_description: PNG page images, one per rendered page

title: Render RST Page Image
cap_description: Render a page-like PNG image preview of the RST document
documentation:
## Purpose

Renders a reStructuredText (RST) file as a page-like PNG image. The RST markup is formatted and laid out, then captured as a PNG. Useful for generating document previews of RST documentation files.

Width of the page image in pixels
Height of the page image in pixels

## Purpose

Renders a plain text file as a page-like PNG image — monospaced font, line-by-line layout. Useful for generating visual previews of text documents, configuration files, or output logs for display in image-aware contexts.

title: Render Page Image
cap_description: Render PNG page images from the file
documentation:
## Purpose

Generic page-image renderer that accepts any supported document type and produces PNG page images. This is the base cap; type-specific variants (PDF, Markdown, text, RST, log) provide more precise media type matching in the capfab.

## Inputs

- A document file (any supported media type).
- Page width in pixels (default 800).
- Page height in pixels (default 1200).
- An optional index range (default: `1-`, all pages).

## Outputs

A sequence of PNG images, one per rendered page.

## Notes

- For typed pipelines, prefer the format-specific render caps ([Render PDF Page Images](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:pdf";op=render_page_image;out="media:image;png"), [Render Markdown Page Image](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:md;textable";op=render_page_image;out="media:image;png"), etc.) so the planner can route correctly.
- This generic variant is useful when the input media type is not known at pipeline design time.

args.arg_description: Path to the document file to process
Width of the page image in pixels
Height of the page image in pixels
Index Range to render from (1-based).

output.output_description: PNG page images, one per rendered page

title: Resolve Model Filters
cap_description: Dry-run a model download to preview which files would be fetched, including any architecture-driven additions on top of the user's filters.
documentation:
## Purpose

Resolves a model spec against the remote repository without downloading anything, returning the full effective file list. The response distinguishes the user's include/exclude filter result from the additions modelcartridge would make based on the detected architecture (`config.json` `model_type`).

This is the dry-run companion of [Download Model](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:model-spec;textable";op=download-model;out="media:download-result;textable;record"). Use it to drive read-only previews in download wizards, where the user's filter is shown alongside the modelcartridge-added mandatory files (e.g. `tokenizer_config.json`, `tokenizer.model`, `special_tokens_map.json`) that are required to load that architecture.

## Inputs

- A model spec string with optional include/exclude filters (e.g. `hf:org/model?include=*.safetensors,*.json&exclude=*-fp32*`).
- Optional HuggingFace bearer token (`hf_token`) for gated repositories.

## Outputs

A JSON record:

```
{
  "model_spec": "hf:org/model?...",
  "applied": {
    "include": ["*.safetensors", "*.json"],
    "exclude": ["*-fp32*"],
    "mandatory_added": ["tokenizer_config.json", "tokenizer.model"],
    "model_type": "mistral",
    "effective_files": [...]
  }
}
```

## Notes

- The resolution result is advisory — the actual download response includes its own `applied_filters` field reflecting the file set used at download time. Compare both to detect repository changes between resolution and download.
- The `mandatory_added` list is empty when the user's filter already covers everything the architecture needs.
- For gated repositories without a valid `hf_token`, the call fails hard with a clear authentication error.

args.arg_description: Model spec to resolve filters for
HuggingFace bearer token. Required when targeting a gated repository.

output.output_description: JSON record describing the architecture-aware effective file list, including any modelcartridge additions on top of the user's filter.

title: Search Models
cap_description: Search the HuggingFace Hub, optionally restricted to a set of architectures.
documentation:
## Purpose

Searches HuggingFace for models matching a query, with optional restriction to a set of `config.json` `model_type` values. Drives the wizard's first page where the user picks a model — the cap that owns the picker forwards its `supported_model_types` set so search results are pre-filtered to runnable models.

## Inputs

A JSON request:

```
{
  "query": "instruct",
  "limit": 20,
  "tags": [],
  "sort": "trending",
  "cursor": null,
  "architectures": ["mistral", "llama", "qwen2"]
}
```

Optional HuggingFace bearer token (`hf_token`) for gated repositories.

## Outputs

A JSON response:

```
{
  "models": [
    {
      "id": "org/model",
      "model_type": "text-generation",
      "model_arch": "mistral",
      ...
    }
  ],
  "returned_count": 20,
  "has_more": true,
  "next_cursor": "...",
  "previous_cursor": null
}
```

## Notes

- `model_type` is the HuggingFace pipeline tag; `model_arch` is the structural architecture from `config.json` and is the field used for cap compatibility checks.
- When `architectures` is empty, no architecture restriction is applied.
- Results without a detectable `model_arch` are filtered out when an architecture restriction is in effect — a cap that requires a specific architecture cannot load a model whose architecture is unknown.

args.arg_description: Search request JSON record
HuggingFace bearer token. Required when targeting gated repositories in search.

output.output_description: JSON record listing matching remote models with detected architecture.

title: Test Edge 1 (Prepend Transform)
cap_description: Transform node1 to node2 by prepending optional text argument
documentation:
## Purpose

Internal test cap. Transforms a node1 text input into a node2 text output by optionally prepending a text prefix. Used to exercise capfab routing, argument passing, and edge traversal in the test suite.

## Inputs

- A node1 text file.
- An optional prefix string to prepend.

## Outputs

The input text with the prefix prepended (or the input unchanged if no prefix).

## Notes

- Not for production use. This cap and its associated media types exist solely for testing the capfab planner and executor.

title: Describe Image (Candle)
cap_description: Generate image descriptions using BLIP models with Candle ML framework
documentation:
## Purpose

Generates a natural-language description of a PNG image using BLIP (Bootstrapped Language-Image Pretraining) models running locally via Candle. Suitable for alt-text generation, image indexing, and content moderation pipelines.

## Inputs

- A PNG image file.
- An optional text prompt to guide the description (e.g. "What objects are visible?" or "Describe the background."). Without a prompt, BLIP produces a general caption.
- A BLIP model spec from HuggingFace. Defaults to `blip-image-captioning-large`.

## Outputs

A text description of the image as a plain string.

## Notes

- BLIP models produce shorter, more caption-like descriptions than vision-language models (LLaVA/Qwen). For detailed, instruction-following descriptions, prefer [Describe Image (GGUF)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:gguf;in="media:image;png";ml-model;op=describe_image;out="media:image-description;textable";vision) or [Describe Image (MLX)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:image;png";ml-model;mlx;op=describe_image;out="media:image-description;textable";vision).
- Input must be PNG.
- For embedding an image into a vector space rather than describing it, use [Generate Image Embeddings (Candle)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:candle;in="media:image;png";ml-model;op=generate_image_embeddings;out="media:embedding-vector;textable;record").

args.arg_description: Path to the image file to describe
Optional prompt to guide the image description
BLIP model name from HuggingFace

output.output_description: Generated text description of the image

title: Describe Image (GGUF)
cap_description: Generate detailed image descriptions using GGUF multimodal models (LLaVA, BakLLaVA) with llama.cpp backend
documentation:
## Purpose

Generates detailed, instruction-following natural-language descriptions of PNG images using GGUF multimodal (vision-language) models via llama.cpp. Supports models like moondream2, LLaVA, and BakLLaVA. The prompt can ask specific questions about image content.

## Inputs

- A GGUF vision model spec (HuggingFace repo or local path). Defaults to `moondream2-20250414-GGUF`.
- A PNG image file.
- A text prompt (default: "Describe this image in detail."). Customize to ask specific questions.
- Max tokens (default 256) and temperature (default 0.6).

## Outputs

A text description of the image as a plain string.

## Notes

- Vision-language models follow instructions in the prompt, making them more flexible than BLIP-style captioning models.
- Lower temperature (0.3-0.5) produces more factual, less creative descriptions.
- For Apple Silicon, prefer [Describe Image (MLX)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:in="media:image;png";ml-model;mlx;op=describe_image;out="media:image-description;textable";vision) for better performance.
- Input must be PNG.

args.arg_description: GGUF vision model specification (HuggingFace repo or local path)
Path to the image file to describe
Text prompt for the image description
Maximum number of tokens to generate
Sampling temperature (0.0-2.0)

title: Describe Image (MLX)
cap_description: Generate detailed image descriptions using MLX-based vision-language models on Apple Silicon
documentation:
## Purpose

Generates detailed, instruction-following natural-language descriptions of images using MLX-optimized vision-language models on Apple Silicon. Supports local file paths and HTTP(S) URLs as input. Delivers fast, energy-efficient vision inference on Mac M-series hardware.

## Inputs

- A model spec (MLX HuggingFace repo or local path). Defaults to `Qwen3-VL-4B-Instruct-4bit`.
- A PNG image file (local path or HTTP(S) URL).
- A text prompt (default: "Describe this image in detail."). Customize to ask specific questions.
- Max tokens (default 256).

## Outputs

A text description of the image as a plain string.

## Notes

- Requires Apple Silicon (M1 or later).
- Qwen3-VL and similar vision-language models can answer detailed questions, read text in images (OCR), identify objects, and describe scenes.
- This cap accepts HTTP(S) URLs directly in the file path argument, unlike the GGUF and Candle variants which require local paths.
- For cross-platform pipelines, use [Describe Image (GGUF)](https://capdag.com/browse/cap:gguf;in="media:image;png";ml-model;op=describe_image;out="media:image-description;textable";vision).
